MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT
Off Calendar May-03-2013 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES: No appearance on this matter. RULING: The Court adopted its tentative ruling. DEFENDANTS METRO CAB LLC & RICHARD HYBELS' MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT is denied. The motion for summary adjudication is granted in part and denied in part. The motion for summary adjudication is granted as to the first and second causes of action for discrimination. Defendant correctly cites Gov't 12926(d) for the proposition that it must "regularly employ[] five or more persons" to be considered an "employer" subject to FEHA and a discrimination claim. Defendant's undisputed material fact 15 establishes that defendant only employed 2 persons. Plaintiff's response to that fact does not cite to any contrary admissible evidence. The Court denies summary adjudication as to the third cause of action for intentional infliction of emotional distress, sixth cause of action for harassment, and the prayer for punitive damages. Pursuant to Gov't Code 12940(j)(1) and (j)(4)(A), Plaintiff can maintain the harassment cause of action even if he is an independent contractor. At pages 10:20-27, 31:2-34:21, 41:21-42:27 of Plaintiff's supplemental declaration, Plaintiff describes conduct that can constitute harassment. Defendant's evidentiary objection is only to the entirety of that document, and is overruled because at least some portions of it are admissible. The Court grants summary adjudication as to the breach of contract and wrongful termination causes of action. Even if the Court assumes that the written contract was forged, Plaintiff has not presented evidence sufficient to create an implied-in-fact contract that could be terminated only for cause. The mere statement at the outset that Plaintiff could keep working as long as he paid the gate fee cannot reasonably be read to limit termination to good cause and is insufficient to overcome the normal rule that working relationships are at will. Moreover, Plaintiff has testified that he had no contract with Defendant at all. In any event, even if the Court assumes an oral contract with a good cause discharge covenant governed the parties' relationship, the undisputed evidence establishes that defendant had good cause to end the relationship. Defendant's undisputed material fact 14 establishes that plaintiff's accidents and increased costs were the basis for the termination. In his opposition statement, Plaintiff does not point to any controverting admissible evidence. ORDER SIGNED AFTER HEARING. Judge: Peter Busch, Clerk: Lesley Fiscella, Not reported.
